snippet: Metformin is substantially excreted by the kidney, and the risk of metformin accumulation and lactic acidosis increases with the degree of renal impairment.

name: Metformin Treatment for Patients with Diabetes and CKD - Fella Health
snippet: Metformin is contraindicated when eGFR falls below 30 mL/min/1.73 m2 due to increased lactic acidosis risk from drug accumulation.

name: FDA: Metformin Safe for Some Patients With Renal Problems
snippet: If the eGFR is below 30 mL/min per 1.73 m2 — defined as advanced renal disease — the drug is contraindicated. Dr Inzucchi is entirely on board ...

name: Metformin is contraindicated when eGFR falls below: A) 30 mL/min B
snippet:
The use of metformin is contraindicated in patients with factors predisposing to lactic acidosis, including impaired renal function.
However, there is an increased relative risk of hospitalization owing to lactic acidosis with metformin in people with CKD G4/5 (ie, eGFR<30mL/ ...
